key_themes: Strong Q1 2026 earnings beat with record EPS of $1.11 (highest in nearly two decades)
Raised full-year net interest income (NII) guidance to 6-8%
Record equities trading revenue and solid investment banking rebound
Widespread analyst price target upgrades (many to $60–$65 range)
Healthy consumer banking, efficiency gains, and stable credit quality
summary: Bank of America delivered outstanding Q1 2026 results, beating EPS ($1.11 vs $1.01 est.) and revenue estimates while posting its highest EPS in almost two decades. Strong equities trading, higher NII, raised full-year guidance, and healthy consumer trends drove the beat and prompted numerous analyst upgrades with price targets mostly above $60. Investor and social media reaction has been clearly bullish, with the stock rising post-earnings and commentary highlighting a strong start to bank earnings season.
sources: 12+ news articles (CNBC, Reuters, Yahoo Finance, GuruFocus, Bank of America IR), 20+ recent X posts (April 15–18, 2026) focused on earnings reaction
